meta.goal: Enable authorised admins to manage Memorioso templates from the Admin Console so template availability, ordering, defaults, visibility, and lifecycle can be controlled without code deploys.

basedOnPriorSprints: Sprint 6 - guided templates and editor direction
Sprint 7 - Layout to PDF Renderer v1
whyNow: Templates are a core product surface and need operational control during alpha and launch
Broken, low-quality, or confusing templates should be hideable immediately without redeploying
Admins need to manage guided templates, default templates, cover templates, and template ordering centrally
Template lifecycle and compatibility with orientation/reflow/PDF rendering need visible governance
productContext: Memorioso depends heavily on templates for ease of use, especially for elder-friendly guided experiences
Template quality directly affects onboarding, book creation, completion rate, and export success
Admin control over templates reduces launch risk and speeds iteration

nonGoals: In-browser WYSIWYG frame editor for template creation
Editing low-level renderer code from admin console
Bulk import/export of templates from third-party marketplaces
Deep localization management for all template copy variants
dependencies: Sprint 45 admin auth and RBAC foundation

operational: Admin can enable or disable a template within seconds without code changes
Admin can change default template ordering and visibility from the console
Broken templates can be removed from user selection safely without affecting existing books
product: Template catalog shown to users stays aligned with current launch/alpha strategy
Admins can clearly distinguish templates intended for guided use versus advanced/freeform use
Template compatibility issues are visible before a template is published
quality: 100% of template admin APIs are permission-guarded server-side
Existing books remain stable when template visibility changes
security: Only authorised roles can manage templates
Non-admin users cannot access template admin operations
Unsafe template state changes are blocked by validation

personas.name: Super Admin
personas.description: Can view, create, edit, publish, disable, reorder, and archive templates and defaults.

personas.name: Content Admin
personas.description: Primary operator for template lifecycle, copy, availability, and presentation.

personas.name: Support Admin
personas.description: May view template metadata for support purposes but should not manage template configuration unless explicitly allowed.

personas.name: Normal App User
personas.description: Regular Memorioso customer. Must never access admin template controls.

inScope: Template status lifecycle such as draft, published, disabled, archived
Cover template and page template metadata management
Compatibility indicators for orientation, reflow, and export support
Safe disable/archive rules for templates already in use
outOfScope: Building new layout frames visually from scratch
Live template preview renderer with full fidelity editing

rules: templates.view is required for templates list and detail access
templates.manage is required for create and edit actions
templates.publish is required for publish/disable actions
templates.reorder is required for changing display order or featured ordering
templates.archive is required for archive actions
Client-side visibility rules must mirror server-side enforcement, not replace it

definitions.draft: Template exists in admin but is not selectable by users.
definitions.published: Template is available to eligible users in the template picker.
definitions.disabled: Template is hidden from new selection but preserved for existing books using it.
definitions.archived: Template is retired from active operations and only retained for historical/admin reference.

transitionRules: draft -> published allowed only if validation passes
published -> disabled allowed at any time
disabled -> published allowed if validation passes
published -> archived should require disable first unless explicit override policy exists

adminSections.purpose: Edit display information, category, and visibility
Show support for orientation, reflow, export, and renderer version
Show whether template is in use and impact of changes

sourceEntities.name: template_versions_optional
sourceEntities.purpose: Track revisions if versioning exists or is added

sourceEntities.name: projects
sourceEntities.purpose: Track usage counts and active use of templates

superAdminUsefulUseCases.useCase: A published template is causing export failures
adminNeeds: prevent new users from selecting it

superAdminUsefulUseCases.useCase: A new guided template is ready for alpha users only
The default starter template should change
adminNeeds: ensure only one default per applicable category/surface

superAdminUsefulUseCases.useCase: Users are confused by too many templates
Template is incompatible with landscape or reflow

userStories.title: Browse template catalog
userStories.iWant: to view all templates with status, type, and usage
userStories.soThat: I can manage the template catalog efficiently

userStories.title: Create or edit template metadata
userStories.iWant: to manage template name, description, category, visibility, and compatibility flags
userStories.soThat: the right templates are presented correctly to users

userStories.title: Publish or disable templates safely
userStories.iWant: to control lifecycle states with validation
userStories.soThat: broken or unfinished templates do not reach users

userStories.title: Manage defaults and ordering
userStories.iWant: to change default templates and display order
userStories.soThat: the best templates appear first for users

userStories.title: Protect existing books from template admin changes
userStories.iWant: template disable/archive actions to avoid breaking existing projects

functionalRequirements.title: Templates list page
requirements: Provide /admin/templates page
Display paginated/searchable template catalog
Allow filtering by status, type, visibility, category, guided level, and compatibility flags
Show key fields such as name, status, type, visibility, order, default, and usage count

functionalRequirements.title: Template detail page
requirements: Provide /admin/templates/:templateId page
Show metadata, lifecycle state, compatibility, usage summary, and audit-friendly timestamps
Support edit mode for authorised roles

functionalRequirements.title: Create template metadata entry
requirements: Allow authorised roles to create a new template record
Required fields should include name, slug, type, status=draft by default, and key compatibility metadata
Creation does not automatically publish template

functionalRequirements.title: Edit template metadata
requirements: Allow editing of name, description, category, visibility, guided level, thumbnail, and compatibility flags
Allow editing cover/page template relationships where supported
Validation must block invalid combinations

functionalRequirements.title: Publish/disable/archive template
requirements: Allow publish only if required metadata and compatibility checks pass
Allow disable even when template is in use, but prevent breaking existing books
Allow archive only under safe policy rules
All lifecycle changes must require confirmation

functionalRequirements.title: Default template management
requirements: Allow setting a template as default for its applicable surface/category
System must enforce only one active default per applicable slot unless multi-default design exists
Changing default should not mutate existing books

functionalRequirements.title: Template ordering
requirements: Allow admins to change display order for template picker surfaces
Ordering should support featured templates first
Ordering changes should reflect in user-facing catalog without redeploy

functionalRequirements.title: Usage visibility and safety checks
requirements: Show how many books currently use a template
Show whether template is safe to archive
Warn when disabling/archiving a heavily used template
Do not allow destructive removal that breaks historical rendering if such dependency exists

functionalRequirements.title: Compatibility visibility
requirements: Show portrait/landscape compatibility
Show reflow compatibility
Show PDF export support
Show renderer/version constraints if applicable
Warn if template is incomplete for supported launch modes

functionalRequirements.title: Permission-aware template admin
requirements: Only templates.view roles can view catalog and detail
Only templates.manage roles can create/edit
Only templates.publish roles can publish/disable
Only templates.reorder roles can change ordering
Only templates.archive roles can archive

functionalRequirements.title: Audit logging
requirements: Log template creation
Log template edit changes
Log publish/disable/archive transitions
Log default changes and ordering updates
Include actor, template id, action, diff summary if feasible, and timestamp

recommendedEndpoints.purpose: Set template as default for its applicable slot

error.message: This template cannot be published until required checks pass.

pages.title: Templates
pages.purpose: Browse, filter, and manage template catalog

pages.title: Template Detail
pages.purpose: Inspect and edit a single template

uxRules: Show compatibility warnings prominently before publish
Use confirmations for publish, disable, archive, and default changes
Display usage count and impact warnings before risky actions
Keep template list highly scannable with status and type badges

validationRules.description: Template cannot be published until name, slug, type, visibility, and required compatibility fields are present
Only one published default template can exist for a given template slot/category unless explicitly configured otherwise
Template cannot be archived if archival would break active historical rendering guarantees
Template flags must not claim support for orientation/reflow/export modes the renderer cannot handle

technicalTasks.title: Define template admin model and validation contract
technicalTasks.description: Document template fields, lifecycle statuses, compatibility rules, and validation requirements.

technicalTasks.title: Build templates list and detail APIs
technicalTasks.description: Implement permission-guarded admin APIs for template listing, filtering, detail retrieval, create, and update.

technicalTasks.title: Build lifecycle action APIs
technicalTasks.description: Implement publish, disable, archive, set-default, and reorder endpoints with validation and safety rules.

technicalTasks.title: Build templates admin UI
technicalTasks.description: Create /admin/templates page with filters, badges, search, and action entry points.

technicalTasks.title: Build template detail and edit UI
technicalTasks.description: Create template detail page with metadata form, compatibility panel, usage panel, and lifecycle controls.

technicalTasks.title: Build ordering and default management UI
technicalTasks.description: Provide simple controls to reorder templates and set defaults safely.

technicalTasks.title: Usage and impact integration
technicalTasks.description: Show usage counts and impact warnings derived from projects using each template.

technicalTasks.title: Audit logging integration
technicalTasks.description: Track all template admin changes and lifecycle actions.

technicalTasks.title: Testing
technicalTasks.description: Add unit, integration, and E2E coverage for template management flows.

metrics: count of validation failures on publish
loggingNotes: Log actor, template id, action, and timestamp
Prefer structured diff summaries for metadata changes
Avoid logging secret/internal-only asset URLs if any exist

securityRequirements: All template admin endpoints must be protected by admin auth and template-specific permissions
Non-admin users must not access template admin routes or APIs
Support admins should remain read-only unless explicitly granted manage rights
Unsafe lifecycle transitions must be blocked server-side
Template pages remain noindex and excluded from sitemap

acceptanceCriteria.then: they can view a filtered list of templates with status, type, visibility, default state, and usage count
the changes are validated, saved, and audit logged

acceptanceCriteria.given: a draft template missing required fields
acceptanceCriteria.when: an admin attempts to publish it
acceptanceCriteria.then: publication is blocked with a clear validation message
new users can no longer select it, but existing books using it remain intact

acceptanceCriteria.when: an authorised admin sets one as default
acceptanceCriteria.then: the system enforces default uniqueness for that slot/category
they can view metadata but cannot edit, publish, reorder, or archive

acceptanceCriteria.when: they attempt to access template admin route or API

unitTests: lifecycle transition rules enforce valid status changes
e2eTests: content_admin opens templates catalog and filters templates
content_admin edits draft template and publishes it after validation passes
negativeTests: unauthorised actor cannot access template admin APIs
invalid reorder payload is rejected safely
attempt to archive unsafe in-use template is blocked
hidden fields are not modifiable without permission

risks.risk: Template changes break user-facing picker or export flows
risks.mitigation: Use strict validation and compatibility checks before publish

risks.risk: Disabling or archiving templates breaks existing books
risks.mitigation: Make disable hide only for new selection and enforce archive safety rules

risks.risk: Too many templates create catalog confusion
risks.impact: Poor user onboarding and lower completion
risks.mitigation: Allow ordering, featured defaults, and admin visibility control

risks.risk: Template admin grows into an uncontrolled low-code editor
risks.mitigation: Keep sprint focused on metadata, lifecycle, defaults, and ordering only

recommendedOrder: Define template schema and validation rules
Integrate usage warnings and audit logs
futureSprintHooks: Future: template performance and conversion analytics

definitionOfDone: Admin templates catalog page exists with filters and statuses
Admins can create and update template metadata
Admins can publish, disable, archive, reorder, and set defaults under permission and validation rules
Usage counts and impact warnings are visible
Automated tests cover lifecycle, validation, permissions, and core UI flows
